entries.definition:
كتاب التاء

تنأ - تهم

(تبع) التاء والباء والعين أصل واحد لا يشذ عنه من الباب شيءٌ، وهو التُّلُوُّ والقَفْو. يقال تبِعْتُ فلاناً إذا تَلَوْتَه [و] اتّبعْتَه. وأتْبَعْتُهُ إذا لحِقْتَه. والأصل واحد، غير أنّهم فَرَقوا بين القَفْو واللُّحُوق فغيَّرُوا البناءَ أدنى تغييرٍ.قال الله: ] فَأَتْبَعَ سَبَباً [ ، [و]: ] ثُمَّ أَتْبَعَ سَبَباً [ فهذا معناه على هذه القراءة اللُّحوق، ومن أهْلِ العربيّة

t–b–ʿ an attachment, to follow, to succeed, to pursue, to abide
by, to comply with; to send after, to cause to go after; a follower,
a creditor; result; liability; consequence; follow up, successor;
uniformity; one’s shadow. Of this root, 13 forms occur 174 times
in the Qurʾan: تَبِعَ tabiʿa nine times; أَتْبَعَ ʾatbaʿa 13 times; أُتْبِعَ
ʾutbiʿa twice; ٱتَّبَعَ ʾittabaʿa 134 times; أُتُّبِعَ ʾuttubiʿa twice; تَابِع
tābiʿ twice; تَابِعِينَ tābiʿīn once; ٱتِّبَاع ittibāʿ twice; مُتَّبَعُون
muttabaʿūn twice; مُتَتَابِعَيْن mutatābiʿayn twice; تَبَع tabaʿ twice; تَبَعٍ

129
entries.definition_english:
t–b–ʿ an attachment, to follow, to succeed, to pursue, to abide
by, to comply with; to send after, to cause to go after; a follower,
a creditor; result; liability; consequence; follow up, successor;
uniformity; one’s shadow. Of this root, 13 forms occur 174 times
in the Qurʾan: تَبِعَ tabiʿa nine times; أَتْبَعَ ʾatbaʿa 13 times; أُتْبِعَ
ʾutbiʿa twice; ٱتَّبَعَ ʾittabaʿa 134 times; أُتُّبِعَ ʾuttubiʿa twice; تَابِع
tābiʿ twice; تَابِعِينَ tābiʿīn once; ٱتِّبَاع ittibāʿ twice; مُتَّبَعُون
muttabaʿūn twice; مُتَتَابِعَيْن mutatābiʿayn twice; تَبَع tabaʿ twice; تَبَعٍ

129

entries.definition:
tabiʿ once and تُبَّع tubbaʿ twice.
تَبِعَ tabiʿa a [v. trans.] 1 to come after, to succeed (2:263) قَوْلٌ
مَّعْرُوفٌ وَمَغْفِرَةٌ خَيْرٌ مِّن صَدَقَةٍ يَتْبَعُهَا أَذًى a kind word [from a would-be
giver] and forgiveness [from the would-be receiver of charity]
are better than almsgiving after which follows hurt 2 to abide by,
to follow, to adhere to, to keep to (2:38) فَمَن تَبِعَ هُدَايَ فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ
وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ those who follow My guidance need not fear nor
grieve.

أَتْبَعَ ʾatbaʿa [v. IV] I [trans.] either: to make someone a
follower, to subjugate; to catch up with, to overtake (7:175) وَاتْلُ
عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ الَّذِي آتَيْنَاهُ آيَاتِنَا فَانسَلَخَ مِنْهَا فَأَتْبَعَهُ الشَّيْطَانُ فَكَانَ مِنَ الْغَاوِينَ
and recite to them the story of the one to whom We gave Our messages, but
he tore himself apart from them, so Satan recruited him as his
follower (or, caught up with him) and he became one of the lost;
*(18:85) فَأَتْبَعَ سَبَبًا so he went after/in search [lit. followed a road]
of [knowledge/wisdom] II [doubly trans.] to cause to come after,
to cause to follow (2:262) الَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَالَهُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ثُمَّ لَا يُتْبِعُونَ مَا
أَنفَقُوا مَنًّا وَلَا أَذًى those who spend their wealth in God’s cause and do
not cause hurt [to the recipient] to follow what they have spent.

أُتْبِعَ ʾutbiʿa [pass. v.] to be followed with, to be pursued (by)
(11:99) وَأُتْبِعُوا فِي هَٰذِهِ لَعْنَةً and they were pursued in this [life] by a
curse.

ٱتَّبَعَ ʾittabaʿa [v. VIII, trans.] 1 to follow (12:108) قُلْ هَٰذِهِ سَبِيلِي
أَدْعُو إِلَى اللَّهِ عَلَىٰ بَصِيرَةٍ أَنَا وَمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَنِي say, ‘This is my way: I call to God
with sure knowledge, I and all who follow me.’ 2 to go after, to go
behind (15:65) فَأَسْرِ بِأَهْلِكَ بِقِطْعٍ مِّنَ اللَّيْلِ وَٱتَّبِعْ أَدْبَارَهُمْ so travel with your
household in a part of the night, and walk behind them 3 to
accompany (48:15) سَيَقُولُ الْمُخَلَّفُونَ إِذَا ٱنطَلَقْتُمْ إِلَىٰ مَغَانِمَ لِتَأْخُذُوهَا ذَرُونَا نَتَّبِعْكُمْ
those who were left behind will say, when you set out after spoils
to capture them, ‘Let us accompany you’ 4 to emulate, to copy, to
repeat (75:18) فَإِذَا قَرَأْنَاهُ فَٱتَّبِعْ قُرْآنَهُ emulate the recitation when We
have recited it.

أُتُّبِعَ ʾuttubiʿa [pass. v. VIII] to be followed (2:166) إِذْ تَبَرَّأَ الَّذِينَ
ٱتُّبِعُوا مِنَ الَّذِينَ ٱتَّبَعُوا when those who have been followed disown
those who followed [them]. 


تَابِع tābiʿ [act. part.] [one] following (2:145) وَلَئِنْ أَتَيْتَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا
الْكِتَابَ بِكُلِّ آيَةٍ مَا تَبِعُوا قِبْلَتَكَ وَمَا أَنْتَ بِتَابِعٍ قِبْلَتَهُمْ وَمَا بَعْضُهُمْ بِتَابِعٍ قِبْلَةَ بَعْضٍ
yet even if you brought every proof to those who were given the
Scripture, they would not follow your prayer direction, nor will
you be following theirs, nor indeed will any of them be following
one another’s prayer direction.

تَابِعِينَ tābiʿīn [pl. of act. part./n. تَابِع tābiʿ] helpers, attendants,
followers, entourage (24:31) وَلا يُبْدِينَ زِينَتَهُنَّ إِلا لِبُعُولَتِهِنَّ ... أَوِ التَّابِعِينَ غَيْرِ
أُولِي الْإِرْبَةِ مِنَ الرِّجَالِ ....
and not reveal their charms except to their
husbands, ..., or such men as attend them who have no sexual
desire ....

اتِّبَاع ʾittibāʿ [v. n.] following, conformity (2:178) فَمَنْ عُفِيَ لَهُ مِنْ
أَخِيهِ شَيْءٌ فَاتِّبَاعٌ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَأَدَاءٌ إِلَيْهِ بِإِحْسَانٍ but if someone [the culprit] is
relieved from some [of the penalty] by his [aggrieved] brother [in
humanity], then there shall be conformity [to his wish] in fair
practice, and restitution [of the remaining part of the penalty] to
him [the aggrieved] in a good way.

مُتَّبَعُون muttabaʿūn [pl. of pass. part. مُتَّبَع muttabaʿ] those
followed, those pursued (44:23) فَأَسْرِ بِعِبَادِي لَيْلًا إِنَّكُمْ مُتَّبَعُونَ so travel
in the night with My servants, for you are sure to be pursued.

مُتَتَابِعَيْن mutatābiʿayn [dual of act. part. مُتَتَابِع mutatābiʿ]
successive, consecutive, following one another without
interruption (4:92) فَمَنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ فَصِيَامُ شَهْرَيْنِ مُتَتَابِعَيْنِ but anyone who
does not find the means must fast for two consecutive months.

تَبَع tabaʿ [v. n. functioning as a coll. n. or as an adj.] followers,
following (14:21) وَبَرَزُوا لِلَّهِ جَمِيعًا فَقَالَ الضُّعَفَاءُ لِلَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا إِنَّا كُنَّا لَكُمْ تَبَعًا
and they [humankind] appeared before God, all together; and the
weak said to those who thought themselves great, ‘We were
followers [or a following] of yours.’

تَبِيع tabīʿ [pass./act. part.] either helper, protector, prosecutor,
one who is prosecuted, or sued, for a right or dues, particularly
for blood revenge (17:69) فَيُرْسِلَ عَلَيْكُمْ قَاصِفًا مِنَ الرِّيحِ فَيُغْرِقَكُمْ بِمَا كَفَرْتُمْ ثُمَّ
لا تَجِدُوا لَكُمْ عَلَيْنَا بِهِ تَبِيعًا and sends against you a hurricane of wind
and drowns you for your ungratefulness, then you will find no
helper for you against Us (or, you shall find none to demand

131
